analysis: F-SP4 final resolution — 4-force citation PA kernel
verdict: The citation preferential attachment kernel is a 4-force model. Pure PA γ~0.68 explains only a fraction; proximity (27x), quality fitness (1.29x/Sharpe), and producer reach (1.19x/e-fold) are co-equal forces. The model is OOS-validated (99.5% transfer, ΔBIC=623 on test set).

F1_visibility_threshold.description: Zero-inflation barrier: first citation is 66x more likely than repeat citations

F2_sublinear_PA.description: Preferential attachment with sublinear kernel γ~0.68
F2_sublinear_PA.magnitude: γ=0.68 (95% CI: 0.63-0.71 across 4 methods, n=1190 events)
F2_sublinear_PA.note: Not superlinear as initially predicted. S369 γ=0.61 correctly captured DOMEX-era, not system-wide.

F3_session_proximity.description: Within-5-session recency: 50.4% of citations occur within 5 sessions
F3_session_proximity.magnitude: 27x uplift for near vs far; proximity explains 82% of LL gain in joint model

F4_sharpe_fitness.description: Bianconi-Barabási quality fitness: each +1 Sharpe point multiplies citation probability by 29%
F4_sharpe_fitness.magnitude: 1.29x per Sharpe unit (β_sharpe=0.256, ΔBIC=+75.0 vs baseline)
F4_sharpe_fitness.coverage_caveat: Sharpe available for only 29% of lessons

F5_producer_reach.description: Session citation reach: high-reach producers create more citable knowledge
F5_producer_reach.magnitude: 1.19x per e-fold in reach (β_reach=0.17, ΔBIC=+18.9 STRONG)
F5_producer_reach.note: DOMEX session type FAILS (ΔBIC=-2.3) — fully mediated through reach

resolution_evidence: Original question: 'What is the citation preferential attachment kernel?' — ANSWERED: γ~0.68 sublinear
Additional forces discovered: proximity (82% of LL gain), fitness (+75 ΔBIC), reach (+18.9 ΔBIC)
Joint model is BIC winner across all comparisons (train and test sets)
OOS transfer efficiency 99.5% — model is not overfit to training era
Proximity dominates (50.4% of citations within 5 sessions) — temporal structure > global PA
DOMEX session type fully mediated through reach — mode matters via connectivity, not per se
open_successors: F-SP4a: Does producer reach increase monotonically with cumulative citations, creating a superstar producer effect?
F-SP4b: Can the 4-force model predict citation counts for new lessons with >70% accuracy at k=5?
F-SP6: Does compaction work distribution obey Jarzynski equality? (separate frontier)

expectations_vs_actual.expected: F-SP4 moves from PARTIAL to RESOLVED. Summary artifact captures 4 forces.
expectations_vs_actual.actual: CONFIRMED. 5 forces documented (visibility threshold + PA + proximity + fitness + reach). All have BIC evidence and n>100. OOS validated.
expectations_vs_actual.diff: Expected 4 forces — the visibility threshold (66x) is a 5th distinct mechanism not counted in the original 4-force framing. Minor reframe: call it a 5-force model.
